title: Derris nesiotes
taxonomy: Derris nesiotes is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The genus Derris is a type of legume, and the species Derris nesiotes is a part of this classification. Derris nesiotes is classified under the subfamily Faboideae and the order Fabales. This classification places Derris nesiotes within the larger family of legumes.
uses: Derris nesiotes, a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia and Australia, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating wounds, skin infections, and other conditions. The plant's extracts have been used to manage fever, headaches, and respiratory issues. Traditionally, Derris nesiotes has also been used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and constipation. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal properties and potential health benefits.

constituents: Derris nesiotes is known to contain several active constituents, including rotenone and desacetylarotenone. These compounds are responsible for its insecticidal properties. Rotenone is a natural insecticide that is toxic to aquatic insects, while desacetylarotenone is a more potent form of rotenone. preparations: Derris nesiotes, a plant native to tropical Asia, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. side_effects: Derris nesiotes, a plant used in traditional medicine, contains rotenone, a toxic compound that can cause side effects.  Ingesting or coming into contact with the plant may lead to skin irritation, allergic reactions, and eye problems. Prolonged exposure to rotenone can also cause neurological damage and other systemic issues. The plant's toxicity is a major concern when handling it, and proper precautions should be taken to minimize exposure. If ingested, Derris nesiotes can cause severe symptoms, including nausea, vomiting, and even organ failure in extreme cases.
intro: Derris nesiotes, also known as the "Tahina" or "Derris of Madagascar," is a plant species in the legume family that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including isoflavones, alkaloids, and glycosides, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
